title: The two-cell P0 projection residual is orthogonal to constants

source.notes: Generated from checked Lean ground truth; source repository withheld during public release.

informal.statement: The residual after subtracting the two-cell average is orthogonal to every constant test value c.
assumptions: u0 and u1 are real cell values
c is a real constant test value
the residual is measured relative to the two-cell average
informal.expected_conclusion: The residual has zero inner product with the constant test value.

declarations.name: cellAverage2
declarations.statement: def cellAverage2 (u0 u1 : Real) : Real := (u0 + u1) / 2

declarations.name: p0Projection2_orthogonal_const
declarations.statement: theorem p0Projection2_orthogonal_const (u0 u1 c : Real) : (u0 - cellAverage2 u0 u1) * c + (u1 - cellAverage2 u0 u1) * c = 0

tasks.input: Translate residual orthogonality to constant test functions for a two-cell P0 projection into Lean 4.
tasks.target: theorem p0Projection2_orthogonal_const (u0 u1 c : Real) : (u0 - cellAverage2 u0 u1) * c + (u1 - cellAverage2 u0 u1) * c = 0

tasks.input: Complete the Lean proof by factoring out c and using the zero-mean residual lemma.
tasks.target: Rewrite the expression as the residual sum times c, then use p0Projection2_residual_mean_zero.

tasks.input: Find the LeanNumerics lemma that turns the residual sum into zero.
